reasoning: The fetch_ts_yfinance tool generated a combined statistical prior of 0.41 for the first resolution date (2025-12-28), indicating that continuous reversion from the recent high of 17.96 pulls the short-term probability below 50%. A crucial structural component is the upcoming combined $0.35 per share dividend ($0.20 regular + $0.15 special) which goes ex-dividend on December 31, 2025. This creates an immediate ~$0.35 mechanical downward drop in the stock price for all resolution dates starting in 2026. However, Host Hotels has a positive longer-term trendline (an upward slope of roughly $0.02 per day) and strong fundamental updates for 2025 (raised EBITDAre guidance). As the forecast horizon extends to January 20 and March 21, the continuous positive linear drift helps overcome the $0.35 expected drop. Furthermore, the expanding volatility over 1-month and 3-month horizons inherently flattens the distribution, pulling the probability of being above the threshold back toward 50%. Thus, my estimates start anchored near the 0.41 statistical estimate for late December, then cleanly regress toward 0.5 over the longer horizons to respect the increasing uncertainty and market drift.
